blocks.text: Allergies: erythromycin base, cefacior, Sulfa (Sulfonamide Antibiotics).
Diagnosis: Dislocation of carpometacarpal joint of right thumb,
F/u po intakes, bm, wt - 2/25 (low risk) dx: R wrist dislocation
- meal schedule, selective menus, nourishments available
Allergies: erythromycin base, cefacior, Sulfa (Sulfonamide Antibiotics),
Diagnosis: Dislocation of carpometacarpal joint of right thumb,
Acetaminophen (Tylenol) 325 MG, Give 650 mg (2 tablet(s)) by mouth Every Six Hours Not to exceed more than 3 g/day from all acetaminophen
Albuterol Sulfate (Albuterol Sulfate) 2.5 MG/3ML, Administer contents of 2.5 mg (3 mL) via nebulizer Every Six Hours PRN hold for HR >100
amiodarone HCL (Pacerone) 200 MG, Give 200 mg (1 tablet(s)) by mouth Daily
Arformoterol Tartrate (Brovana) 15MCG/2ML Administer contents of 15 mcg (2 mL) via nebulizer Twice a Day Give with Budesonide. (Do not
order upon DC, formulary substitute for home Symbicort) Indication: asthma
atenoloL (Tenormin) 50 MG, Give 100 mg (2 tablet(s)) by mouth Daily Hold for SBP less than 110 or HR less than 60
atorvastatin CALCIUM (Lipitor) 40 MG. Give 40 mg (1 tablet(s)) by mouth at Bedtime
bisacodyL (Dulcolax) 10 MG, Give 10 mg (1 suppositor(ies)) rectally Daily PRN PRN Use 3rd for Bowel Evacuation Indication: bowel
Budesonide (Budesonide) 0.5 MG/2ML, Administer 0.5 mg (2 mL) via nebulizer Twice a Day Give with Arformoterol. Rinse mouth after use (Do
not order upon DC, formulary substitute for home Symbicort) Indication: Asthma
Diclofenac Sodium (Voltaren) 1 %, Apply GEL (GRAM) topically Three Times a Day B/I knees
Upper body dose= 2.25 inches long (2 grams)
Lower body dose= 4.5 inches long (4 grams) Indication: pain
Furosemide (Lasix) 20 MG, Give 20 mg (1 tablet(s)) by mouth Daily
Montelukast Sodium (Singulair) 10 MG, Give 10 mg (1 tablet(s)) by mouth at Bedtime
polyethylene glycoL 3350 (Miralax) 17G, Give 17 gram (1 packet(s)) by mouth Daily PRN PRN Use 1st for Bowel Evacuation Indication:
Rivaroxaban (Xarelto) 10 MG, Give 20 mg (2 tablet(s)) by mouth In the evening "DO NOT CRUSH*
HIGH ALERT MED Indication: deep venous thrombosis
tiZANidine HCL (Zanaflex) 4 MG, Give 2 mg ( 1/2 tablet(s)) by mouth Every Eight Hours PRN Indication: muscle spasm
Zinc Oxide (Zinc Oxide) 20% Apply OINTS. (G) topically As needed PRN Indication: skin irritation
